functions.name: bohrium_open_sdk.opensdk._base_client.retry
documentation:
Decorator for retrying a function upon exception.

max_retries: The maximum number of retries before giving up.
delay: Initial delay between retries in seconds.
backoff: Multiplier applied to delay between retries (e.g., for exponential backoff).
exceptions: Tuple of exceptions to catch. Defaults to base Exception class.

variables.name: bohrium_open_sdk.opensdk._base_client.DefaultHttpxClient
documentation:
An alias to `httpx.Client` that provides the same defaults that this SDK
uses internally.

This is useful because overriding the `http_client` with your own instance of
`httpx.Client` will result in httpx's defaults being used, not ours.

methods.name: bohrium_open_sdk.opensdk._base_client.SyncAPIClient._prepare_request
documentation:
This method is used as a callback for mutating the `Request` object
after it has been constructed.
This is useful for cases where you want to add certain headers based off of
the request properties, e.g. `url`, `method` etc.

classes.name: bohrium_open_sdk.opensdk._base_client.APIResponseManager
documentation: class APIResponseException(Exception):
def __init__(self, message: str, response: Optional[httpx.Response] = None):
self.message = message
self.response = response
self.code = response.status_code if response else None

class_variables.name: bohrium_open_sdk.opensdk._exceptions.APIError.body
documentation:
The API response body.

If the API responded with a valid JSON structure then this property will be the
decoded result.

If it isn't a valid JSON structure then this will be the raw response.

If there was no response associated with this error then it will be `None`.
